en_body:
...I know not since when I can no longer understand even myself! Before, I always commanded myself in nearly every circumstance. I was self-confident and exacting. I could choose, therefore I could hold the situation firmly in my hands and compose my own position!
 ...Now, since loving him: I have not one particle of self-command left! Emotion like enraged waves pounds and buries me, and I am wholly feeble to resist. The suffering is that, accustomed to directing my own feelings, I tend naturally to struggle against this new current of emotion: and always it is so: I am exhausted!
 ...I never know what I shall feel, even one moment from now! And Love, in the middle season of age, suddenly wears so strange a face! I thought I had felt every emotion that can be felt, except the final moment of a human life: but he uproots me from even myself!
 ...Then so: this is Love! A very new experience: though sometimes piercing with pain, yet a human life passing beyond its season now suddenly discovers realms of emotion so miraculous, so mystical—perhaps this too is a gracious blessing bestowed at random by Life: Thank You, my Love!
note: The poem treats midlife Love as both dispossession of the self and an unforeseen blessing from Life.
